name: I just got hurt and can't do my job anymore—what are my first steps?
description: Immediately report the injury to your supervisor and seek medical attention. Obtain a written list of work restrictions from your doctor and provide them to you

mainEntity.name: I just got hurt and can't do my job anymore—what are my first steps?

acceptedAnswer.text: Immediately report the injury to your supervisor and seek medical attention. Obtain a written list of work restrictions from your doctor and provide them to your employer to determine if they can accommodate your limitations or if you qualify for disability benefits.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If your workplace injury prevents you from returning to your specific job roles, you should immediately notify your supervisor and seek a formal medical evaluation. In Utah and Idaho, your doctor must provide a 'work status' report outlining specific restrictions. If the employer cannot accommodate these restrictions (e.g., 'no lifting over 10 lbs'), you are typically eligible for Temporary Total Disability (TTD) benefits. You must ensure every communication regarding your inability to work is documented in writing.

Key factors include the specific medical restrictions set by your physician and whether your employer has 'light duty' available.

1. Report the injury and your inability to perform tasks immediately.
2. Get a written restriction list from your doctor.
3. Provide this list to your HR or supervisor and keep a date-stamped copy.

Having a legal advocate ensures the insurance carrier doesn't 'deem' you fit for work when your body says otherwise.
